Package com.here.sdk.navigation

Enum Class RoadSignType

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<RoadSignType>
com.here.sdk.navigation.RoadSignType
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<RoadSignType>, Constable
public enum RoadSignType extends Enum<RoadSignType>

A road sign type classifying road signs that can appear along a road. Some signs are standardized and look the same in all countries, e.g. STOP_SIGN. In general, the visual appearance of the road signs can differ across countries. Some road signs can be combined with other signs, like WeatherType signs. The road sign will be always shown topmost.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class java.lang.Enum

    Enum.EnumDesc<E extends Enum<E>>

    Enum Constant
    Description
    ACCIDENT_HAZARD
    A sign indicating accident hazard.
    ANIMAL_CROSSING
    A sign indicating animal crossing.
    BICYCLE_CROSSING
    A sign indicating bicycles crossing.
    CONGESTION_HAZARD
    A sign indicating congestion hazard.
    CROSSING_WITH_PRIORITY_FROM_THE_RIGHT
    A sign indicating crossing with priority from the right.
    DOUBLE_HAIRPIN
    A sign indicating a double hairpin.
    EMBANKMENT
    A sign indicating embankment.
    END_OF_ALL_RESTRICTIONS
    A sign indicating the end of all restrictions.
    END_OF_LOW_GEAR
    A sign indicating the use of low gear.
    END_OF_NO_ENGINE_BRAKE
    A sign indicating the end of no engine brake zone.
    END_OF_NO_OVERTAKING
    A sign indicating the ending of a no overtaking zone.
    END_OF_NO_OVERTAKING_TRUCKS
    A sign indicating the end of no overtaking trucks zone.
    FALLING_ROCKS
    A sign indicating falling rocks.
    FLOOD_AREA
    A sign indicating a flood area.
    GENERAL_CURVE
    A sign indicating a general curve.
    GENERAL_HILL
    A sign indicating a general hill.
    GENERAL_WARNING_SIGN
    A sign indicating a general warning.
    HORN_SIGN
    A sign indicating restriction for horning.
    HUMP_BRIDGE
    A sign indicating a hump bridge.
    ICY_CONDITIONS
    A sign indicating icy conditions.
    LANE_MERGE_CENTER
    A sign indicating merging of the center lane.
    LANE_MERGE_LEFT
    A sign indicating merging of the left lane.
    LANE_MERGE_RIGHT
    A sign indicating merging of the right lane.
    LATERAL_WIND
    A sign indicating lateral winds.
    LOW_GEAR
    A sign indicating the use of low gear.
    NO_CAMPER_OR_MOTORHOME_ALLOWED
    A sign indicating no camper or motorhome allowed.
    NO_ENGINE_BRAKE
    A sign indicating no engine brake.
    NO_IDLING
    A sign indicating no idling.
    NO_TOWED_CARAVAN_ALLOWED
    A sign indicating no towed caravan allowed.
    NO_TOWED_TRAILER_ALLOWED
    A sign indicating no towed trailer allowed.
    NO_TURN_ON_RED
    A sign indicating no turning on red permitted.
    OBSTACLE
    A sign indicating an obstacle.
    PEDESTRIAN_CROSSING
    A sign indicating pedestrian crossing.
    PRIORITY_OVER_ONCOMING_TRAFFIC
    A sign indicating priority over oncoming traffic.
    PROTECTED_OVERTAKING_EXTRA_LANE
    A sign indicating an extra lane for overtaking.
    PROTECTED_OVERTAKING_EXTRA_LANE_LEFT_SIDE
    A sign indicating an extra lane for overtaking on the left side.
    PROTECTED_OVERTAKING_EXTRA_LANE_RIGHT_SIDE
    A sign indicating an extra lane for overtaking on the right side.
    RAILWAY_CROSSING_PROTECTED
    A sign indicating a protected railway crossing.
    RAILWAY_CROSSING_UNPROTECTED
    A sign indicating an unprotected railway crossing.
    RISK_OF_GROUNDING
    A sign indicating risk of grounding.
    ROAD_NARROWS
    A sign indicating a narrowing road.
    SCHOOL_ZONE
    A sign indicating school zone.
    SHARP_CURVE_LEFT
    A sign indicating a sharp curve to the left.
    SHARP_CURVE_RIGHT
    A sign indicating a sharp curve to the right.
    SLIPPERY_ROAD
    A sign indicating slippery road.
    START_OF_NO_OVERTAKING
    A sign indicating the starting of a no overtaking zone.
    START_OF_NO_OVERTAKING_TRUCKS
    A sign indicating no overtaking trucks.
    STEEP_HILL_DOWNWARDS
    A sign indicating a steep hill downward.
    STEEP_HILL_UPWARDS
    A sign indicating a steep hill upwards.
    STOP_SIGN
    A sign indicating a stop.
    TRAMWAY_CROSSING
    A sign indicating a tramway crossing.
    TRIPLE_HAIRPIN
    A sign indicating a triple hairpin.
    TRUCK_ROLLOVER
    A sign indicating truck rollover.
    TURN_PERMITTED_ON_RED
    A sign indicating turning on red permitted.
    TWO_STAGE_LEFT
    A sign indicating that turning left requires a two-stage maneuver, also known as a hook turn or Copenhagen Left, which is a special maneuver to safely make a left turn at an intersection without crossing oncoming traffic.
    TWO_STAGE_RIGHT
    A sign indicating turning right with the specified vehicle type requires a two stage maneuver.
    TWO_WAY_TRAFFIC
    A sign indicating two way traffic.
    UNEVEN_ROAD
    A sign indicating uneven road.
    UNKNOWN
    Unknown road sign type
    URBAN_AREA
    A sign indicating urban area.
    WINDING_ROAD_STARTING_LEFT
    A sign indicating a winding road starting left.
    WINDING_ROAD_STARTING_RIGHT
    A sign indicating a winding road starting right.
    YIELD
    A sign indicating yielding.
    YIELD_TO_BICYCLES
    A sign indicating yielding to bicycles.
    YIELD_TO_ONCOMING_TRAFFIC
    A sign indicating yielding to oncoming traffic.

    • NO_CAMPER_OR_MOTORHOME_ALLOWED

      public static final RoadSignType NO_CAMPER_OR_MOTORHOME_ALLOWED

      A sign indicating no camper or motorhome allowed. Example: No examples available.

    • NO_TURN_ON_RED

      public static final RoadSignType NO_TURN_ON_RED

      A sign indicating no turning on red permitted. Example: No turn on red sign

    • TURN_PERMITTED_ON_RED

      public static final RoadSignType TURN_PERMITTED_ON_RED

      A sign indicating turning on red permitted. Example: Turn permitted on red sign

    • TWO_STAGE_LEFT

      public static final RoadSignType TWO_STAGE_LEFT

      A sign indicating that turning left requires a two-stage maneuver, also known as a hook turn or Copenhagen Left, which is a special maneuver to safely make a left turn at an intersection without crossing oncoming traffic. This maneuver applies only in right-hand driving countries and is particularly beneficial for cyclists, as it minimizes interaction with oncoming traffic, allowing for safer crossings. Example: Two stage left turn

    • TWO_STAGE_RIGHT

      public static final RoadSignType TWO_STAGE_RIGHT

      A sign indicating turning right with the specified vehicle type requires a two stage maneuver. A TWO_STAGE_RIGHT maneuver, is a special maneuver commonly used by cyclists to safely make a right turn at an intersection without crossing oncoming traffic. This maneuver is applicable only in left-hand driving countries and is particularly beneficial for cyclists as they allow for safer crossings by minimizing the interaction with oncoming traffic. Example: Two stage right turn

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static RoadSignType[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static RoadSignType valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null
